description: A local narrative unit composed of tightly related Events. Episodes are formed from short-range temporal continuity, shared participants, tightly coupled actions, or explicit near-range causal progression. They organize local narrative movement rather than abstract thematic analysis.

output_fields.name: description
output_fields.description: Neutral narrative prose grounded in the text, describing concrete actions and observable outcomes without thematic or purposive analysis.

output_fields.name: related_events
output_fields.description: List of Event ids that belong to this Episode.

output_fields.name: related_occasions
output_fields.description: List of Occasion ids that contextualize or anchor this Episode.

construction_constraints: Only entities of type Event may appear in related_events.
Each Event must belong to exactly one Episode within a single extraction result.
Occasion may contextualize multiple Episodes but must never appear in related_events.
Each Episode should contain 3 to 8 Events whenever possible; create a singleton Episode only when meaningful grouping is not possible.
Prefer grouping Events that belong to the same dramatic beat, continuous interaction, or short contiguous action segment, especially when they share the same Occasion or core participants.
Do not split a continuous conversation or action run into multiple Episodes unless a clear local shift occurs, such as a new conflict, a new objective, or a visible change of situation.
Do not invent new Events, Occasions, motivations, causal links, or psychological states.
writing_constraints: The description must be neutral narrative prose and must not explain the grouping rationale.
Do not use meta-narrative phrases such as 'this episode shows' or 'this sequence centers on'.
Do not include thematic analysis, purpose analysis, or structural commentary.
The Episode name should summarize the main action progression rather than an abstract theme.
The description should cover the local setup, the key progression, and the immediate outcome in one coherent paragraph when the text supports it.

samples.name: Guards Detect the Intrusion and Raise the Alarm
samples.description: While on patrol, the guards notice signs of an unauthorized entry, alert nearby staff, and trigger the base alarm. The area quickly shifts into a full security response.

description: A persistent narrative thread spanning multiple Episodes. A Storyline summarizes a longer-range causal or conflict progression at a higher level for downstream narrative understanding and reasoning.

output_fields.name: name
output_fields.description: A concise title capturing the overall Storyline progression.

output_fields.name: description
output_fields.description: A coherent synopsis covering the initiating trigger, the progression, and the resulting outcome.

output_fields.name: impact
output_fields.description: The reusable downstream consequence, constraint, or new state produced by this Storyline.

output_fields.name: key_characters
output_fields.description: Canonical names of key characters that recur prominently in the Storyline.

output_fields.name: key_locations
output_fields.description: Canonical names of key locations that anchor the Storyline.

construction_constraints: Build the Storyline only from the provided Episode chain information and do not use external knowledge.
The Storyline must summarize the whole chain instead of paraphrasing a single Episode.
Do not add characters, locations, events, or causal links not supported by the input.
writing_constraints: The description should cover cause, process, and result in one coherent narrative.
The impact should express a reusable downstream state or constraint for later reasoning.
Do not reference Episode numbers, scene numbers, or segment numbers.
Do not output bullet lists or step-by-step enumeration.

samples.name: System Failure Forces a Full Evacuation
samples.description: A cascading system failure destabilizes the base and triggers a series of emergency responses. Local containment efforts fail to restore control, and the command staff eventually order a full evacuation as the danger spreads through the facility.
samples.impact: The operating order of the base collapses, forcing all subsequent action to unfold under higher risk and tighter resource constraints.
